articles.title: The Apple of haircare gets its German cheque
articles.description: Ruka, the London-based textured-hair company founded by Zimbabwean entrepreneur Varaidzo Tendai Moyo and her co-founder Ugorji Agbai, has closed a $4.5m round co-led by Henkel Ventures and Freedom Trail Capital. The deal, which takes lifetime funding to roughly $10m and brings one of the world's largest consumer-goods groups onto the cap table, is the most meaningful endorsement yet of a Zimbabwean diaspora-founded brand.

articles.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
articles.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist's day-long procession through the city's campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice.

articles.title: Protecting lions and people: the biologist dedicated to tackling human-wildlife conflict
articles.description: Moreangels Mbizah has blazed a trail in Zimbabwe as the first black African woman to found a conservation organisation in the country

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: President Mnangagwa Appoints Elizabeth Gwaunza as Zimbabwe’s First Female Chief Justice
more.description: President Mnangagwa Appoints Elizabeth Gwaunza as Zimbabwe’s New Chief Justice President Emmerson Mnangagwa has appointed Elizabeth Gwaunza as Zimbabwe’s new Chief Justice. Her elevation to the top judicial post makes her the first woman in the country’s history to lead the judiciary. Justice Gwaunza takes over from Luke Malaba, who is retiring after serving at [...] The post President Mnangagwa Appoints Elizabeth Gwaunza as Zimbabwe’s First Female Chief Justice appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: The ZEP Trap: Why South Africa’s New Permit Extensions Could Be a Deportation Time Bomb
more.description: For hundreds of thousands of Zimbabweans living and working in South Africa, the recent announcement extending the Zimbabwe Exemption Permit (ZEP) to May 2027 might have initially felt like a significant reprieve. Yet, beneath the surface of this apparent concession lies a chilling possibility: that this ‘extension’ is not a genuine act of compassion but [...] The post The ZEP Trap: Why South Africa’s New Permit Extensions Could Be a Deportation Time Bomb first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Luke Malaba’s Final Bow: The Secret ‘Constitutional Deals’ That Kept the Chief Justice in Power
more.description: Harare – As Chief Justice Luke Malaba finally prepares to vacate his high-profile office on 14 May 2026, the celebratory headlines mask a decade of judicial turbulence that has shaken the foundations of Zimbabwean democracy. This article goes far beyond the official retirement announcements to dissect the “Malaba Legacy”—a period defined by the highly controversial [...] The post Luke Malaba’s Final Bow: The Secret ‘Constitutional Deals’ That Kept the Chief Justice in Power first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Gwaunza appointed Chief Justice as Malaba retires
more.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has appointed Justice Elizabeth Gwaunza as Chief Justice following the retirement of Chief Justice Luke Malaba. Gwaunza who had been serving as Deputy Chief Justice becomes the first Zimbabwean female Chief Justice. In a statement issued Thursday, Chief Secretary to the President and Cabinet Martin Rushwaya announced the appointment which takes effect [...] The post Gwaunza appointed Chief Justice as Malaba retires appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.description: HARARE – Attorney General Virginia Mabhiza has declared that there is no legal basis for demanding a referendum to pass the Constitution (Amendment No. 3) Bill, 2026, putting her at direct odds with constitutional lawyers and opposition parties. Mabhiza, speaking to journalists on Wednesday days before parliament is is set to vote on the bill, [...] The post Attorney General insists CAB3 referendum an ‘unconstitutional demand’ appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .
